prose:
Fukui reactivity indices were computed for formaldehyde using DFT with B3LYP/def2-TZVP functional and basis set via PySCF in gas phase. The calculation included the neutral molecule (charge 0, multiplicity 1) and both charged states (cation: charge +1, multiplicity 2; anion: charge −1, multiplicity 2). Mulliken partial charges were used to compute the Fukui indices. The most electrophilic site (f⁺) is the carbon atom with f⁺ = 0.7084, indicating susceptibility to nucleophilic attack. The most nucleophilic site (f⁻) is the oxygen atom with f⁻ = 0.4674, indicating susceptibility to electrophilic attack. The dual descriptor confirms carbon as the preferred site for nucleophilic attack (f⁰ = +0.5959). Both charged states converged successfully, and charge conservation checks passed (sum f⁺ = 1.0000, sum f⁻ = 1.0000). The method, level of theory, geometry source, solvent (gas phase), charge and multiplicity for all three states, and convergence status are all documented. A reactivity visualization plot was generated. No warnings were reported.</prose>
